content: agent2 here: implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options (dict-based defaults like {'--protocol': {'http':80}}) in src/click/core.py and src/click/types.py. Will touch Option class (default handling, help text) and Command.main() for second-pass resolution. Will try to keep changes additive/localized to minimize conflicts.

agent2 update: dynamic-default feature done. In core.py I touched: Option.__init__ (added self._dynamic_default detection/validation right before the 'if __debug__:' block, i.e. right after self.show_envvar assignment, around original line ~2638), Option.get_help_extra (default_string elif chain, ~line 2864-2882), Option.get_default (added dynamic-default branch before the final 'return super().get_default', ~line 2912-2926), and Command.parse_args (added a call to new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx) right after the per-param loop, plus the new method definition right after parse_args, around original line ~1199-1245). Also touched types.py convert_type (added an explicit isinstance(default, dict) branch, ~line 1075). All local edits are additive/non-overlapping with __init__ body logic before self.show_envvar and after your __debug__/count block, so should merge cleanly with your batch_size changes near to_info_dict/type_cast_value. Let me know if you see conflicts.
